attributes.action: Reverse the freeze on the student loan repayment threshold for Plan 2 loans
attributes.additional_details: In the 2025 Autumn Budget, the Government announced that the student loan repayment threshold would be frozen from April 2027. This use of fiscal drag will mean graduates on Plan 2 loans in England who earn above the threshold repay more of their income during a cost of living crisis and on top of the tax threshold freezes. This Labour government should ease the burden of student debt, not increase it.
attributes.background: The Government should reverse its decision to freeze the student loan repayment threshold from April 2027 for Plan 2 loans. It should also consider reducing the repayment rate from 9% and increasing the repayment threshold immediately.

rejection.details: You may wish to sign this petition which calls for similar action: https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/759293
